content.full_text:
THE CITY OF

SAN DIEGO

CITY ADMINISTRATION BUILDING • 202 C STREET • SAN DIEGO, CALIF 92101

CHARLES G. ABDELNOUR, J.D.
City Clerk, C.M.C.

Office of the
CITY CLERK
533-4000

October 22, 1990

Kevin Braaten-Moen
Fair Political Practices Commission
Technical Assistance Division
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

Dear Mr. Braaten-Moen:

This is to follow up on the telephone conversation between you
and Joyce Lane of my staff. That discussion centered on whether
the Together for Linda Bernhardt Committee (Statement of
Organization attached) is required to file a campaign statement
in accordance with section 84200.5f of the Political Reform Act,
in connection with the petitions to recall Ms. Bernhardt which
were filed in my office on October 9, 1990.

It is my understanding, based on your conversation with Ms. Lane,
that the Bernhardt Committee is not required to file this
statement since the committee was organized as a candidate
committee, not as a committee primarily formed or existing "to
support or oppose the qualification of a ballot measure."

You informed Ms. Lane that only qualification committees are
required to file the statement in accordance with the above
referenced section, and that it is permissible for an
officeholder to use campaign funds to fight a recall effort -
without incurring any responsibility to file this special
campaign statement.

Since my office is responsible for these filings, in accordance
with the Political Reform Act, I am requesting that you confirm
this information in writing for our records.

Thank you for your attention to this matter.

Sincerely,

Charles G. Abdelnour
City Clerk

◎ Printed on recycled paper

sections.question_synthetic: The City Clerk requests written confirmation from FPPC regarding whether the 'Together for Linda Bernhardt Committee' (a candidate committee organized to oppose a recall petition) is required to file a campaign statement under section 84200.5 of the Political Reform Act.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from a city clerk seeking written confirmation of prior FPPC telephone advice. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections present. Document references a prior telephone conversation between FPPC staff (Kevin Braaten-Moen) and the requester's staff (Joyce Lane). Request is dated October 22, 1990.

embedding.summary: The City Clerk of San Diego sends a follow-up letter to FPPC seeking written confirmation of prior telephone guidance that the Together for Linda Bernhardt Committee is not required to file a campaign statement because it is a candidate committee rather than a qualification committee, and that officeholders may use campaign funds to fight recall efforts.
